back_translation: The word of the LORD came to Jonah son of Amittai, saying,
"Get up, go to Nineveh the great city, and cry out proclaiming-judgment against that city, because their evil has come up before me."

But Jonah got up to flee to the city of Tarshish, away from the presence of the LORD. He went down to the city of Joppa and found a ship that was about to go to the city of Tarshish. So he paid the fare and boarded the ship to go with the crew, heading to the city of Tarshish, away from the presence of the LORD.
But the LORD caused a great storm-wind to blow upon the sea, and a violent storm arose in the sea, until the ship was about to break apart.

The sailors were afraid, and each one cried out to his own god. They threw the cargo in the ship into the sea to lighten the ship. But Jonah had gone down to the lowest part of the ship and was lying fast asleep.
The captain came to him and said, "How can you be sleeping? Get up, cry out to your god. Perhaps God will consider us, and we will not perish."
Then they said to one another, "Come, let us cast lots so that we may know on whose account this calamity has come upon us." So they cast lots, and the lot fell on Jonah.
Then they asked him, "Tell us, please: on whose account has this calamity come upon us? What is your occupation? Where do you come from? What is your country, and from what people are you?"
He answered them, "I am a Hebrew. I worship the LORD, the God of the heavens, who made the sea and the dry land."
The sailors were exceedingly afraid, and said to him, "Why have you done this?" For they had already known that he was fleeing from the presence of the LORD, because he had told them so himself.
Then they asked him, "What shall we do to you, so that the sea will calm down for us?" For the sea was raging more and more violently.
He answered them, "Pick me up and throw me into the sea, and the sea will calm down for you. For I know that this great storm has come upon you because of me."
Nevertheless, the sailors rowed with all their strength to return to land, but they could not, for the sea was raging more and more violently against them.

So they cried out to the LORD and said, "O LORD, please do not let us perish on account of this man's life, and do not put the guilt of innocent blood upon us. For you are the LORD; you do whatever you are pleased to do."
Then they picked up Jonah and threw him into the sea, and the sea ceased its raging.
Then those sailors greatly feared the LORD, and they offered a sacrifice to the LORD and made vows to him.
